narrative.headline: What 2017 Honda Pilot owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine-related complaints dominate owner reports for this model, with the most common symptom being sudden loss of acceleration accompanied by transmission warning messages while driving at highway speeds. Electrical system faults are the second most frequently reported area, with owners describing multiple warning lights and system failures — including power steering and the speedometer — appearing simultaneously at startup. Powertrain and fuel system complaints round out the picture, with some owners reporting transmission-related acceleration failures and others encountering physical obstructions that prevent normal refueling.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle suddenly failing to accelerate when the accelerator pedal is depressed at highway speeds, often accompanied by a transmission warning indicator and a message instructing them to stop driving. · 3 fire

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners describe multiple warning lights and system errors — including power steering and speedometer failures — appearing simultaneously and without warning when starting the vehicle, with some cases recurring after service. · 2 fire · 1 crash

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report loss of acceleration while driving at highway speeds alongside illumination of the transmission warning indicator and a displayed message to stop driving. · 1 crash

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report finding ice lodged between fuel nozzle components that prevents normal refueling and must be physically removed before the tank can be filled. · 1 crash
